abstract: Comprehensive authoring guide for the Swirls DSL. Covers file structure (twenty-two top-level declarations plus the optional `version:` line: schema, form, webhook, schedule, workflow, stream, view, trigger, secret, auth, postgres, database, migration, disk, skill, agent, mcp, channel, connection, action, role, policy), workflow declarations (the keyword is `workflow`; `graph` is a legacy alias), all 18 node types (agent, ai, bucket, code, database, disk, email, http, integration, map, parallel, postgres, scrape, stream, switch, wait, while, workflow), inline `subgraph { }` blocks for map/while, embedded TypeScript/JSON/SQL/Prisma blocks, the context object including `context.iteration.*` (item/index/input/previous) and `context.db.*` (managed-database client), resources, triggers, versioned top-level stream blocks (a `version` pointer plus a `versions:` map carrying per-version schema/condition/prepare) read by version-pinned `type: stream` nodes, top-level `view` blocks that compose streams into a spreadsheet (a `columns` @ts mapping per source row plus optional `computed` columns that run a graph per row via `input`/optional `output` @ts, surfaced in the cloud UI with per-cell loading states), reusable top-level `schema` blocks referenced by bare identifier, form `visibility:` (public/internal) and HTTP Basic gating via `auth:`, webhook shared-secret authentication via `secret:` + `header:`, identity-scoped access control (`role`/`policy` blocks; declaring policy grants flips the project to deny-by-default, and the former `access` block is removed), agent blocks with tools, profiles, subagent `team` delegation, and optional `wallet` for Zero tool spend, agent `mcp` slots referencing top-level `mcp` blocks bound to remote MCP servers per project in Cloud (tools discovered at runtime as `mcp__<slot>__<tool>`), `channel` blocks binding an agent to Slack / Linear / Discord / web, `connection` blocks declaring Swirls-brokered outbound OAuth slots (slack/linear/discord/linkedin/microsoft) referenced by http nodes and channels (replacing the removed `cloud` auth type), a Swirls-managed `database` primitive (Prisma-language `@prisma` schema island — models and enums only, no datasource/generator/connection URLs — provisioned and migrated by Swirls, queried via a generated typed client on `context.db.<name>`) with a companion `migration` block for ordered data transforms and a `type: database` node that narrows the client to a declared operation (query/insert/update/delete/transaction) for governed, reviewable, traced mutations, distinct from the bring-your-own `postgres` block, reviews, failure policies, output `format:`, and the real lexer hazards (regex literals with quote characters, stray characters at DSL level, hyphenated object keys) with an exhaustive validator-diagnostic cheatsheet.
